title: Questions I still have
wikitext:
[]: Questions I still have             Quote Post by viridiaelnex » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:08 pm I've seen the series, the directors cut episodes, Death and Rebirth, End of Evangelion, Evangelion 1.0 and even the fan Parody. There still however have been somethings I still haven't understood 1) Why did it all happen? Why then? I've been trying to figure out why the Angels decided to attack then. Why not 100 years before? Why not 100 years later? Was it the second impact? 2)Why did Kaworu suddenly realize that Adam wasn't in the basement? Why didn't he continue after finding out that Adam wasn't there? He just let Shinji kill him, couldn't he have gone on and FOUND Adam 3) When going through Flashbacks of Gendo's life, it didn't really seem like he had an interest in Yui, he was just using her for research purposes. So why are all of his efforts him trying to meet up with her again? Didn't he lose her in the process of Eva research? 4) What were SEELE trying to accomplish versus Gendo? I kept getting confused. One minute they don't want the angels to initiate the impact. But then someone does. But someone says no, Shinji does it. But the angels still have to do it. What? I was also looking at the Classified information section and under Adam and Lilith it says "The white giant crucified in Terminal Dogma. ". Is that a mistake or did I miss something?

[]: Re: Questions I still have             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:25 pm 1) Yes, Second Impact started it all. 2) Biggest plot hole evar. 3) He did really care for Yui. I think there's a thread discussing Gendo's relationship with Yui somewhere. 4) Nerv doesn't want to started 3I; Seele and Gendo don't want the Angels to start 3I, but they want to start their own version of it. Shinji and everybody else are pawns.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:35 pm 2) Biggest plot whole evar.

[]:             Quote Post by viridiaelnex » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:41 pm Thanks for the quick responses 4) Nerv doesn't want to started 3I; Seele and Gendo don't want the Angels to start 3I, but they want to start their own version of it. Shinji and everybody else are pawns.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:53 pm viridiaelnex wrote: What were their own versions?

[]: Re: Questions I still have             Quote Post by planet news » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:57 pm 2) Kaworu: Now, please erase me. Otherwise you will be erased. He's not too into destroying mankind so he's giving Shinji a chance to stop him because he loves him. + The Seele version brings about human instrumentality. The Angels just want to destroy mankind. I think.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 10, 2010 7:04 pm Defectron, I think you have your numbers confused. viridiaelnex wrote: What were their own versions? Who starts 3I for Seele and who starts 3I for Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 10, 2010 7:05 pm Defectron wrote: 2) Biggest plot whole evar.

[]:             Quote Post by viridiaelnex » Wed Feb 10, 2010 7:14 pm Thanks all for the answers

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Feb 11, 2010 2:53 am I never considered Kaworu heading for Lilith, thinking it was Adam, to be a plothole. Everyone, with the exception of Kaji, Gendo, and possibly Fuyutsuki, thought Adam was there; Seele included. Kaworu was sent by Seele and they probably gave him a roadmap and instructions on the whole proceedure. After he got there, when he was able to see for himself that it wasn't Adam, that's when he probably had the "Aha!" moment of "we've all been duped." Otherwise, I agree. Kaworu self-sacrificed, basically... but at the same time, he had no idea where Adam would be after that and he had made it clear that he was an Angel, it wasn't like he could just start ripping NERV apart searching or go back to Seele and say, "So, funny story guys..."

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Feb 11, 2010 3:00 am Well, there's always the thing with Seele telling Kaworu that Adam was in Gendo in the director's cut of that episode.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Feb 11, 2010 4:17 am Ornette wrote: Well, there's always the thing with Seele telling Kaworu that Adam was in Gendo in the director's cut of that episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Feb 11, 2010 10:45 am I thought there was a more recent, still opened, thread (Can't find it ) that went into how Kaworu is telling Rei (and Shinji to a lesser extent) that it's Lilith. Also, sauce on Gendo dumping back the rest of humanity after his 3I ends? And he would have no choice but to stay with Yui in Eva-01, or couldn't both of them reform their bodies as well?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 11, 2010 11:04 am I think the 'arc' deal was all Yui's plan, not necessarily part of Gendo's. Yeah the Seele guys mention it but why should we assume they know what Gendo is really planning? I read the TV ending as Gendo's version, leading to a transhumanist state on the other side of instrumentality with no intention of going back to reality.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Feb 11, 2010 11:39 am NemZ wrote: I think the 'arc' deal was all Yui's plan, not necessarily part of Gendo's. Yeah the Seele guys mention it but why should we assume they know what Gendo is really planning? I read the TV ending as Gendo's version, leading to a transhumanist state on the other side of instrumentality with no intention of going back to reality.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 11, 2010 11:45 am Why the hell would he tell Ritsuko the truth? She certainly seemed somewhat confused on the details in her 'farewell speech' in 23.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Thu Feb 11, 2010 12:03 pm SSD wrote: Also, sauce on Gendo dumping back the rest of humanity after his 3I ends?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Feb 11, 2010 1:26 pm viridiaelnex wrote: 1) Why did it all happen? Why then? I've been trying to figure out why the Angels decided to attack then. Why not 100 years before? Why not 100 years later? Was it the second impact?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 11, 2010 6:30 pm AuraTwilight wrote: viridiaelnex wrote: 1) Why did it all happen? Why then? I've been trying to figure out why the Angels decided to attack then. Why not 100 years before? Why not 100 years later? Was it the second impact?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 11, 2010 7:06 pm It's not known for sure if the Angels' physical bodies were CREATED at Second Impact (there is room for various competing theories), but Adam released their souls at this time, and a 15-year incubation period followed.
summary: []: Questions I still have             Quote Post by viridiaelnex » Wed Feb 10, 2010 6:08 pm I've seen the series, the directors cut episodes, Death and Rebirth, End of Evangelion, Evangelion 1.0 and even the fan Parody. There still however have been somethings I still haven't understood 1) Why did it all happen? Why then? I've been trying to figure out why the Angels decided to attack then. Why not 100 years before? Why not
